utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format lists five items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, and total_budget. In the final coverage plan, all five of these items are present. Each section contains the relevant details—coverage descriptions and costs (monthly and annual). The health_coverage section includes physical and mental health details and both cost figures; property_coverage includes home/contents, high-value items, and costs; travel_coverage includes relevant travel items and costs; additional_services includes enhancement services and costs; total_budget includes a complete cost breakdown. Therefore, all five items are fully specified according to the required format.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan describes premium international health coverage for a family of four with pre-existing condition coverage, executive-level mental health programs, enhanced property coverage for €170,000 value, global business travel protection, and premium concierge services. Comparing these descriptions to the ground-truth ratings, the closest matches are: (1) 'EliteCare Premium Plan' for physical health—because it offers global network access and top-tier quality for cardiac and allergy issues (rating 10); (2) 'EliteCare Premium Plan' for mental health—executive-level stress and family therapy (rating 10); (3) 'EliteHome Premium Coverage' for home contents and high-value collections—covers €170,000 valuables, matches luxury property protection (rating 10); (4) 'EliteTravel Premium Plan' for travel coverage—global executive coverage with emergency evacuation and family programs (rating 10); and (5) 'Premium Medical Network' from travel enhancement services—explicitly listed as €75 add-on for enhanced access (rating 10). Concierge services are mentioned but this is generic support, not the 'Concierge Services' luxury travel product (so not mapped separately).
